steps.label: Pre-flight
steps.explanation: Checks that Docker Desktop is running, the NVIDIA Container Toolkit is installed, and your RTX 5080 is accessible inside containers via GPU passthrough. Without this, no training can begin.

steps.label: Metrics Server
steps.explanation: Starts a FastAPI server on your Windows machine that reads metrics.json written by the training container and serves them to this dashboard in real time via REST polling and Server-Sent Events (SSE).

steps.label: Docker Build
steps.explanation: Builds the training Docker image: Ubuntu 22.04, CUDA 12.8 toolkit, Miniforge (conda), Python 3.11, PyTorch 2.7+cu128, Unsloth (fast LoRA), and the full HuggingFace training stack. First run: 10–20 min. Subsequent runs use Docker layer cache (~30s).

steps.label: PDF Extraction
steps.explanation: Parses 2 SPC textbooks (Statistical Process Control + The Book of SPC) using PyMuPDF and pdfminer, then generates question-answer pairs formatted for instruction fine-tuning. Output: /output/spc-qa-pairs.jsonl.

steps.label: LLM Download
steps.explanation: Downloads the Llama-3.2-3B base model from HuggingFace Hub (~6 GB). This only happens on the first run — subsequent runs load from the spc_training_hf_cache Docker volume. Unsloth loads it in 4-bit quantization to fit in 17 GB VRAM.
steps.detail: Downloading unsloth/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct (~6 GB, cached after first run)...

steps.label: Apply LoRA
steps.description: Inject LoRA adapter layers into the model
steps.explanation: Applies LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) to the attention layers of Llama-3.2-3B. This adds ~4M trainable parameters on top of the frozen 3B base — only these adapter weights will be updated during training, keeping memory usage low and training fast.

steps.label: LoRA Training
steps.explanation: Fine-tunes the model using SFTTrainer (Supervised Fine-Tuning). The RTX 5080's 17 GB VRAM handles the 4-bit base model + LoRA adapters with gradient checkpointing. Watch the Loss curve drop — lower is better. Training completes in ~20–60 minutes depending on dataset size.

steps.label: GGUF Export
steps.explanation: Exports the LoRA adapter (not the full model) to GGUF format. The resulting spc-adapter-lora.gguf file (~50–80 MB) is committable to git and loadable on the Jetson Orin Nano alongside the base model using llama-server.

steps.label: Ready for Jetson
steps.explanation: The SPC specialist adapter is ready. Commit it to git and pull on the Jetson Orin Nano 8GB. The spc_agent will load it via llama-server as a LoRA adapter on top of the base Llama-3.2-3B model.
